title: The Early Renaissance in Florence (including painting, sculpture and architecture) (quiz)
topic_path: The Early Renaissance in Florence (including painting, sculpture and architecture) (quiz)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Who commissioned the sculptures for Orsanmichele's exterior niches?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9ec0007fe95ab9b2889326bd4700d3b1.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Many of the guilds of Florence were responsible for commissioning a sculpture for a niche in the exterior wall of this building.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fra Filippo Lippi's *Madonna and Child with two Angels* shows the influence of Renaissance Humanism in its. . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f93321dc2bd745afdc2ece7b3042093.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This *Madonna and Child* painting is more earthly than  previous Madonna and Child paintings, revealing a more Humanist approach by the painter.

choices.content: playful and earthly approach to the subject matter.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Donatello studied classical sculpture and the way that ancient artists represented the body, but also imbued *St. Mark* with . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/212c204da744d054fba66384d7ed79d5.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Donatello studied classical sculpture and the way that ancient artists represented the body, but also imbued St. Mark with distinctly human behavior and natural human movement.

choices.content: a likeness to the Emperor Charlemagne

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which tool developed by Brunelleschi was widely adopted by painters in 15th century Italy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Brunelleschi's experiment led to the development of linear perspective. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7e79016c41b74de59546fe7c220ed707.gif)

Perspective diagram of Leonardo da Vinci's *Last Supper* (1498) with the vanishing point at the head of Christ and the orthogonals (visible in the walls and ceiling) meeting at that single vanishing point.

choices.content: lost wax method of casting bronze

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are the classical elements of Donatello's *David*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f07bc00771cfe824872545020da86f19.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: *David* is a nude in a contrapposto pose, both of which are classical elements (borrowed from ancient Greek and Roman sculpture). In fact, Donatello's *David* is the first free-standing nude since classical antiquity. And the use of bronze also recalls ancient Greek and Roman sculpture.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true of Masaccio's *Tribute Money*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3a2b286f9c02d9ed1977560687a3e9c.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This fresco, in the Brancacci Chapel in Florence, uses both atmospheric and linear perspective to create a convincing illusion of space, in fact it is one of the first paintings to use linear perspective. Masaccio also made the light and shadows in the painting align with the window in the Brancacci Chapel (so the light appears to come from the right). At this time, the Florentine government had just initiated a new tax (the Catasto) and this painting may show that Christ condoned that kind of civic responsibility. 

There is no hierarchy of scale and Masolino painted other scenes in the chapel, but not this one.

choices.content: At this time, the Florentine government had just initiated a new tax and this painting may show that Christ condoned that kind of civic responsibility.
This scene is one of the earliest surviving examples of linear perspective.
Masolino was responsible for the figure of Christ, though Masaccio painted the rest of the fresco.
Masaccio uses hierarchy of scale to indicate the most important figures.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did the Florentine architects have a such a difficult time constructing the dome for the cathedral?** 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/58f585ffa118ee7c74371e0c6b88c0aa.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The city could not get enough lumber at the size needed to construct the wooden scaffolding to make the dome in a traditional way (with wooden centering) and domes are particularly difficult to construct.

choices.content: The city could not get enough lumber at the appropriate sizes to construct the wooden scaffolding to make the dome in a traditional way.
The knowledge for how to vault such a wide space had been lost during the Middle Ages.
Florentine architects could not find enough concrete to make the dome.
The marble for the dome was particularly expensive.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The placement of all the figures in the same space in Domenico Veneziano's *St. Lucy Altarpiece* borrows from an earlier tradition.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fe5e1eba1e488b8795e00fa13ec10ce1.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Earlier polyptychs generally show saints occupying separate panels from the Virgin and child. In Veneziano's painting, all the figures occupy the same architectural space, creating a more believable image — a trend we see emerging in the Renaissance. 

Here's an example of an earlier polyptych with the figures in separate panels.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/377f8049bf35f2b5c48556d368b998b9.jpeg)

Francesco d'Antonio de Ancona, *Madonna and Child with Christ and Saints,* late 14th century (The Pushkin State Museum of Fine Arts, Moscow)
